title: Map bass response to macro motion

tests.description: bass energy > 0 produces greater shader uniform displacement scaling than bass energy = 0
tests.reason: Currently skipped. Original test reads CPU-side particle positions via getParticlePositions(), but all displacement now happens GPU-side in vertex shaders. Must be redesigned to verify that shader uniforms (uBassEnergy, uDisplacementScale) and mesh-level transforms (rotation.y) are correctly driven by bassEnergy parameter.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
tests.approach: Create a particleField, init it, then call draw() with bassEnergy=0 and bassEnergy=0.8. After each draw, read the ShaderMaterial uniforms (uBassEnergy) and the mesh rotation.y. Assert that with bassEnergy=0.8: (a) uBassEnergy uniform equals 0.8, (b) mesh rotation.y includes a bass-driven component (bassEnergy * motionAmplitude * 0.1 * sin(elapsed*0.0003)), (c) expansion uniform effect is present via uBassEnergy > 0. Compare mesh rotation magnitudes between the two cases.
assertions: shaderMaterial.uniforms.uBassEnergy.value equals the bassEnergy passed in params
pointsMesh.rotation.y with bassEnergy=0.8 has larger absolute magnitude than with bassEnergy=0 (after multiple frames)
uDisplacementScale is set to motionAmplitude * structureComplexity (non-zero when bass is active)

tests.description: smoothed bass changes less frame-to-frame than raw bass with jittery input
tests.reason: Already passing. Validates EMA smoothing (factor 0.88) in renderLoop.ts by feeding alternating high/low frequency data and checking that bassEnergy passed to geometry system has smaller consecutive diffs than raw input would produce.
acceptanceCriteria: Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
tests.approach: Existing test uses mock AnalyserPipeline with alternating 200/50 values and asserts maxConsecutiveDiff < 0.4 (raw diff would be ~0.52).
assertions: maxConsecutiveDiff between smoothed bassEnergy values across frames is less than 0.4

tests.description: when pipeline reports all-zero frequency data (muted), bassEnergy is still > 0
tests.reason: Already passing. Validates the synthetic bass fallback (30 + 25 * sin(elapsed * 0.001)) that activates when rawBass < 5, ensuring visuals remain alive when muted.
acceptanceCriteria: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
tests.approach: Existing test feeds all-zero frequency data through the render loop and asserts that bassEnergy passed to geometry system is non-zero in at least some frames.
assertions: bassValues array has at least one entry
at least one bassEnergy value is greater than 0

tests.description: with maximum bass (255), shader uniforms and mesh transforms remain bounded after many frames
tests.reason: Currently skipped. Original test checked CPU-side positions for 0-1 bounds, but displacement is GPU-side. Must be redesigned to verify that: (a) shader uniforms stay in expected ranges at max bass, (b) mesh-level transforms (rotation, position.z) remain bounded, (c) no NaN/Infinity in any uniform value.
acceptanceCriteria: No clipping or visual instability occurs
tests.approach: Create a particleField, init it, then call draw() 200 times with bassEnergy=1.0. After each frame, read shaderMaterial.uniforms and pointsMesh rotation/position. Assert all values are finite, uBassEnergy <= 1.0, rotation.y is bounded by motionAmplitude * 0.1 + 0.15, position.z is bounded by 0.3 * motionAmplitude.
assertions: All uniform values are finite (not NaN, not Infinity) across 200 frames
uBassEnergy never exceeds 1.0
pointsMesh.rotation.y absolute value stays within expected bounds (bass rotation + drift rotation)
pointsMesh.position.z absolute value stays within 0.3 * motionAmplitude
uBreathScale stays within [0.97, 1.03] range

tests.description: bass-driven motion respects prefers-reduced-motion (motionAmplitude=0.2 reduces mesh transform magnitudes)
tests.reason: Currently skipped. Original test compared CPU-side positions. Must be redesigned to compare mesh-level rotation.y and shader uniform uMotionAmplitude between full motion (1.0) and reduced motion (0.2) cases, since vertex shader multiplies all bass deformation by `ma` (uMotionAmplitude).
acceptanceCriteria: No clipping or visual instability occurs
Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
tests.approach: Create two particleFields with identical seeds. Run both for 10 frames with bassEnergy=0.8 but motionAmplitude=1.0 vs 0.2. Compare mesh rotation.y magnitudes and verify the reduced case has proportionally smaller values. Also verify uMotionAmplitude uniform is correctly set.
assertions: uMotionAmplitude uniform equals 0.2 for reduced-motion field
uMotionAmplitude uniform equals 1.0 for full-motion field
Cumulative absolute rotation.y across frames is smaller for reduced-motion than full-motion
uDisplacementScale for reduced-motion (0.2 * complexity) is less than full-motion (1.0 * complexity)

tests.description: no forbidden storage APIs accessed during bass-driven rendering
tests.reason: Already passing. Validates privacy requirement by spying on localStorage and cookie APIs during bass-driven particle rendering and mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
tests.approach: Existing test spies on Storage.prototype.getItem/setItem and document.cookie get/set, runs 20 frames of particle drawing with high bassEnergy, then asserts none of the spies were called.
assertions: Storage.getItem not called
Storage.setItem not called
document.cookie getter not called
document.cookie setter not called

tests.description: bassToMacro mapping produces correct 0-1 range with power curve
tests.reason: No direct unit test exists for the bassToMacro mapping function. Should verify the power curve (exponent 0.7) normalization across boundary values.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
tests.approach: Call mapSignalsToVisuals with bass values of 0, 1, 128, 255 and verify the resulting bassEnergy matches expected power curve output: 0 -> 0, 128 -> (128/255)^0.7 ≈ 0.587, 255 -> 1.0^0.7 = 1.0. Also verify negative inputs clamp to 0.
assertions: bass=0 produces bassEnergy=0
bass=255 produces bassEnergy close to 1.0 (within 0.001)
bass=128 produces bassEnergy close to 0.587 (within 0.02)
bassEnergy is always in [0, 1] range
bass=-10 produces bassEnergy=0

tests.description: camera motion scales orbit radius by bass energy
tests.reason: cameraMotion.ts multiplies orbit displacement by (1 + bassEnergy * 0.15). No dedicated test validates this bass scaling factor.
acceptanceCriteria: Bass band influences at least one large-scale motion property
tests.approach: Import initCameraMotion, updateCamera, and _clearHarmonicCache. Init with a fixed seed, then call updateCamera at a fixed elapsed time with bassEnergy=0 and bassEnergy=1.0. Compare camera.position offsets. The bass=1.0 case should have position offsets scaled by 1.15x relative to bass=0 case.
assertions: Camera position with bassEnergy=1.0 has larger offset from base (0,0,5) than with bassEnergy=0
The ratio of offsets approximates 1.15 (within tolerance for floating point)
Camera position with bassEnergy=0.5 produces intermediate offset
motionAmplitude=0 produces zero displacement regardless of bassEnergy

tests.description: synthetic bass fallback produces time-varying signal within expected range
tests.reason: The synthetic fallback formula (30 + 25 * sin(elapsed * 0.001)) should produce values in [5, 55] range. Should verify the fallback is correctly bounded and time-varying.
acceptanceCriteria: Effect remains visible when audio is unmuted or muted
tests.approach: Use startLoop with a mock pipeline returning all-zero frequency data. Collect bassEnergy values across many frames with varying elapsed times. Verify values are always > 0 and the mapped bassEnergy (via bassToMacro) stays within expected bounds. Verify values change across frames (not constant).
assertions: All bassEnergy values across frames are greater than 0
bassEnergy values vary across frames (not all identical)
No bassEnergy value exceeds bassToMacro(55) ≈ 0.30 (upper bound of synthetic range after smoothing converges)
No bassEnergy value is less than bassToMacro(5) ≈ 0.03 after initial ramp-up frames

tests.description: EMA smoothing converges toward stable value with constant input
tests.reason: Smoothing factor 0.88 should cause exponential convergence. With constant input, smoothed value should reach within 5% of target after ~25 frames.
acceptanceCriteria: Response is smoothed to avoid jitter
tests.approach: Use startLoop with a mock pipeline returning constant frequency data (e.g., all bins at 180). Run 30+ frames and verify that bassEnergy converges toward bassToMacro(180). Check that early frames show ramp-up behavior (starting from 0) and later frames stabilize.
assertions: First frame bassEnergy is significantly less than final frame bassEnergy (ramp-up)
After 25+ frames, bassEnergy is within 5% of the expected converged value
Consecutive frame differences decrease over time (convergence)
